$schema_note: Optimized-branch (Tasks 1-6) staging A/B comparison data. Mirrors shape of 2026-04-28-lc-1644-bench-only-staging-runs.json. Compare run-by-run to that file.

runs.label: first run post-redeploy of optimized branch
runs.notes: First bench after deploying feat/lc-1644-appevent-perf to staging. Iter 0 = 4688ms is the exact post-redeploy Lambda cold-start spike pattern seen in baseline run 14 (4899ms) — NOT a regression. sa_http_ms=0 on iter 0 is suspicious — likely the very-first call routed through a path where the SA timing wasn't captured (perhaps SA call failed to land an http hook on first invocation), with the 4236ms ending up in sa_issue_ms instead. Iter 1+ shows the optimized branch's warm-path improvements clearly. Iter 19 has an isolated didauthvp stall (62ms) — same anomaly type seen in baseline. Per-phase Task 4 instrumentation populating with real numbers (parallel_reads, owner_and_sa_reads, sa_issue, log_activity_and_send_boost).

runs.label: second run, Lambda warm from run 1
runs.notes: Lambda is no longer post-redeploy fresh. Iter 0 = 895ms (vs run 1's 4688ms post-redeploy spike, vs baseline typical cold ~1150ms). sa_http=282ms on iter 0 — visible cold-start tail but dramatically less than baseline run-14's 4011ms extreme. sa_didauthvp=199ms on iter 0 (brain-side DID-Web cache initialization). From iter 1 onwards: warm-steady running at ~290ms p50 — even better than run 1. Iter 10 again has the non-SA spike (456ms total, sa_http=49ms warm, log_activity_and_send_boost=353ms) — same pattern as run 1's iter 10 — suggests there may be a recurring slow-path in logActivity/sendBoost at this iteration cadence worth diagnosing.

runs.label: third run, warm Lambda, captured a mid-run recycle
runs.notes: Iter 0 = 840ms (warm Lambda cold path, similar to run 2's 895ms). Warm-steady p50 = 286ms — best yet. Captured TWO interesting events: (1) iter 5-6 looks like a true mid-run Lambda recycle: sa_http jumps to 262ms then 753ms, with iter 7-9 recovery showing slightly elevated sa_http (67/127/111ms) before settling — exact pattern from baseline run 10. (2) iter 11 parallel_reads=128ms (Neo4j slow query) with otherwise warm SA. Per-phase breakdown lets us cleanly identify which subsystem caused each anomaly — exactly what Task 4 instrumentation was supposed to enable. Even with the recycle event, p50 is dramatically better than baseline.

runs.label: fourth run, clean
runs.notes: Cleanest run yet — no mid-run recycle, no Neo4j slow query. Iter 0 = 835ms (consistent with runs 2/3 typical cold). Iter 1 = 436ms with sa_didauthvp=55 + owner_and_sa_reads=83 — partial cold sub-instance pattern, same type seen in baseline. Warm-steady p50 = 287ms. sa_http p50 = 49ms — slightly under baseline's ~50ms.

runs.label: fifth run, clean with iter-11 parallel_reads spike pattern
runs.notes: Iter 0 = 819ms cold (consistent with run-2/3/4 typical cold). Iter 1 = 435ms lukewarm (parallel_reads=111ms — Neo4j connection still warming). NOTABLE PATTERN: iter 11 again shows parallel_reads spike (112ms here, 128ms in run 3 iter 11) — appears to be a recurring event at this cadence. Could be a periodic Neo4j keepalive, connection refresh, or background task. Worth investigating in follow-up. Otherwise warm-steady p50 = 298ms — within run-1-to-4 cluster (286-308ms). No mid-run Lambda recycle this time.

runs.label: sixth run, slightly elevated logActivity/sendBoost
runs.notes: Iter 0 = 689ms — best cold-path observed yet on optimized branch. Warm-steady p50 elevated to 326ms (vs runs 2-5 cluster of 286-308). The slowdown is isolated to log_activity_and_send_boost (p50=202 vs 173-183 in prior runs). SA path (sa_http p50=52ms) and Neo4j reads (parallel_reads p50=29ms, owner_and_sa_reads p50=30ms) unchanged. Suggests brain-side variability in logActivity DB writes or sendBoost overhead. Iter 3: 477ms with log_activity_and_send_boost=353ms (non-SA spike, anomaly type from baseline).

runs.label: seventh run, best warm-steady yet
runs.notes: Best warm-steady run on optimized branch: p50=281ms. Iter 0=686ms (matches run 6's 689ms — best cold cluster). Iter 1=558ms partial cold sub-instance (sa_http=329ms — Lambda routed to less-warm container). After iter 1, iters 2-19 are very tight: 16 of 18 warm iterations under 305ms. log_activity_and_send_boost p50=168ms — confirms run 6's elevated 202ms was transient brain-side variance, not a sustained pattern.

runs.label: eighth run, mid-run sub-instance scatter at iter 10-12
runs.notes: Tied with run 7 for best warm-steady (p50=280ms). Iter 0=715ms cold. Iter 7 has isolated sub-instance (sa_http=177ms) without follow-on. Iter 10-12 caught a recovering sub-instance scatter (sa_http 165→125→99→64) — milder than run 3's 753ms recycle but same anomaly type. Otherwise extremely consistent: 14 of 20 warm iters under 300ms, log_activity_and_send_boost p50=169ms.

runs.label: ninth run, mixed anomalies
runs.notes: Iter 0=675ms — new best cold-path observed. Warm-steady p50=296ms. This run captured all four classic anomaly types: iter 2 sa_http=189ms (partial cold sub-instance), iter 9 log_activity=343ms (non-SA spike), iter 11 parallel_reads=78ms (Neo4j slow query — recurring iter-11 pattern from runs 3 and 5), iter 14-15 sa_http 116→78 (recovering sub-instance). Iter 8 has decoupled didauthvp pattern (sa_http=45ms warm but owner_and_sa_reads=40ms elevated). Despite the anomalies, p50 still under baseline by ~48%.

runs.label: tenth and final run, lowest warm-steady p50
runs.notes: Final run for the 10-run / 200-iteration dataset. New low warm-steady p50 = 279ms. Iter 0=727ms (consistent typical cold cluster). Iter 2 sub-instance hit (sa_http=252ms isolated event). Iter 16 non-SA spike (log_activity_and_send_boost=338ms). Otherwise extremely consistent: 16 of 18 post-iter-1 iterations under 300ms.

warm_sa_http_max_ms.note: Task 3 keepAlive working — Lambda recycles dramatically less impactful

phase_budget_check.interpretation: Per-phase instrumentation (Task 4) accounts for ~97% of warm total — vs baseline's ~510ms unaccounted (89% of total). The optimized branch closes the budget.

anomaly_observations_optimized.true_mid_run_lambda_recycle: 1 in 200 iterations (run 3 iter 6: 975ms total, sa_http=753ms — half the magnitude of baseline's 3346ms max)
anomaly_observations_optimized.partial_cold_sub_instance: ~1-2 per run (sa_http 116-329ms isolated events) — same frequency as baseline but smaller magnitude
anomaly_observations_optimized.non_sa_spike: ~1 per 30 iterations (log_activity_and_send_boost 230-353ms) — same frequency as baseline. Now isolatable to specific phase via per-phase instrumentation
anomaly_observations_optimized.decoupled_didauthvp_stall: ~3 in 200 iterations (sa_didauthvp 14-62ms with warm sa_http) — same frequency as baseline. Independent of optimizations as expected (brain-side jose/DID-Web cache)
anomaly_observations_optimized.post_redeploy_cold: Run 1 iter 0: 4688ms (vs baseline run 14: 4899ms) — fundamental Lambda issue, not addressed by optimizations. Subsequent runs settle to typical 675-895ms cold.

interim_observations_after_run_1.convergence_with_warm_steady: yes — iter 1 (366ms) within ~60ms of warm steady median (308ms). Task 5 LRU cache populating on iter 0 working as designed.
interim_observations_after_run_1.cold_iter0_interpretation: Same post-redeploy Lambda cold-start spike seen in baseline run 14. Not a regression of the optimized branch. Need more runs (post-Lambda-warm) to characterize typical cold iter 0.

phase_breakdown_warm_steady.phase_sum_p50_ms: 30 + 28 + 64 + 183 = 305ms — accounts for ~99% of warm total (308ms). Per-phase instrumentation now closes the budget that was previously ~510ms unaccounted on baseline.
phase_breakdown_warm_steady.interpretation: log_activity_and_send_boost (~183ms) is now the dominant phase. Task 6 fire-and-forget likely already trimmed this from ~250ms+ to 183ms. owner_and_sa_reads + parallel_reads = 58ms parallel — Task 4 working.

anomalies_observed: iter 19: sa_http=50ms (warm) but sa_didauthvp=62ms — same 'decoupled didauthvp stall' anomaly type seen ~3 in 270 baseline iterations. Independent of optimizations, brain-side jose/DID-Web cache miss. Expected.

iter 10: 467ms (vs ~300ms median) with warm SA call (sa_http=58ms). Time is in log_activity_and_send_boost=347ms (vs ~180ms typical). Same 'non-SA spike' anomaly type from baseline. Now we can SEE that the spike is in logActivity/sendBoost, not Neo4j reads or SA — exactly the per-phase visibility Task 4 promised.

iter 0: sa_http_ms=0 with sa_issue_ms=4236ms. Suggests the post-redeploy cold-start time accumulated outside the http portion of sa_issue (likely TLS handshake / undici pool initialization for the very first call). Task 3 keepAlive should warm the pool from iter 1 onwards — and indeed iter 1 sa_http drops to 72ms.
next_steps: Run more 20-iter benches once Lambda is fully warm to characterize typical cold iter 0 (expect ~700-900ms based on Task 3 mitigation prediction)
Capture 5-10 more runs across times of day to build n>=100 warm-steady iterations
Compare nearest-rank percentiles vs baseline once n is large enough
